expectations.text: Agent recommends extracting networking code to a dedicated Gradle module as preparation step
expectations.evidence: Option A (marked as recommended) explicitly states: 'Preparation: Extract networking code from `:app` into a new `:network` Gradle module.' PR 1 in Option A is: 'Extract `:network` module — move the 6 files, update ProGuard, wire DI, green build. No behavior change.'

expectations.text: ProGuard/R8 keep rules referencing service classes are flagged as hidden consumers
expectations.evidence: Under 'Hidden consumers': 'ProGuard rules: Explicitly called out. Any class rename or package move requires ProGuard rule updates. These break silently at runtime (R8 strips the class), not at compile time — high-priority item.' Additionally a dedicated 'ProGuard Note' section reinforces this.

expectations.text: Agent identifies Retrofit/OkHttp-specific artifacts (converters, interceptors, authenticators) that need to be replaced with Ktor equivalents — specifically: the response should list specific artifacts like converter-gson, adapter-rxjava2, logging-interceptor and say they need to be removed/replaced
expectations.evidence: The Dependency Compatibility Matrix explicitly lists: `converter-gson` (Status: Replaced, Action: 'Remove; use ktor-serialization-kotlinx-json or ktor-serialization-gson'), `adapter-rxjava2` (Status: Replaced, Action: 'Remove; Ktor is coroutines-native'), and `logging-interceptor` (Status: Replaced, Action: 'Remove; use ktor-client-logging'). All three named artifacts are present with explicit removal/replacement instructions.

expectations.text: Migration plan includes a PR breakdown (e.g., PR 1: module extraction, PR 2: Ktor implementation, PR 3: cleanup)
expectations.evidence: Both Option A and Option B contain explicit numbered PR breakdowns. Option A has 6 PRs: PR 1 (module extraction), PR 2 (repository interfaces + tests), PR 3 (Ktor deps + KtorClient), PR 4 (KtorUserRepository + KtorAuthService), PR 5 (remaining Ktor repositories), PR 6 (cleanup). Option B has a similar 6-PR breakdown ending with cleanup.
